title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher

input_row.title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher
input_row.description: The TLS (Transport Layer Security) protocol secures the transmission of data over the internet using standard encryption technology. App Service apps use TLS 1.2 for the Minimum Inbound TLS Version by default and allow for the use of TLS versions 1.0, 1.1, and 1.3. NIST strongly suggests the use of TLS 1.2 and recommends the adoption of TLS 1.3.
input_row.rationale: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are outdated and vulnerable to security risks. Since TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 provide enhanced security and improved performance, it is highly recommended to use TLS 1.2 or higher whenever possible.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that Minimum Inbound TLS Version is set to 1.2 or higher. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to get the TLS version setting: az webapp config show --resource-group --name --query minTlsVersion Ensure that "1.2" or higher is returned. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list apps: Get-AzWebApp Run the following command to get the app in a resource group with a given name: $app = Get-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name Run the following command to get the TLS version setting: $app.SiteConfig.MinTlsVersion Ensure that the command returns 1.2 or higher. Repeat for each app. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: f0e6e85b-9b9f-4a4b-b67b-f730d42f1b0b - Name: 'App Service apps should use the latest TLS version'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, click the drop-down next to Minimum Inbound TLS Version and select 1.2 or higher. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: az webapp config set --resource-group --name --min-tls-version <1.2|1.3> Remediate from PowerShell For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName -Name - MinTlsVersion <1.2|1.3>
input_row.impact: Using the latest TLS version may affect compatibility with clients and backend services.
input_row.default_value: By default, TLS version is set to 1.2.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elastic_beanstalk_application_minimum_tls_version_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls:**
   - Use `boto3` to interact with AWS Elastic Beanstalk applications.
   - Specific methods include:
     - `boto3.client('elasticbeanstalk').describe_apps()` to list all applications.
     - `boto3.client('elasticbeanstalk').describe_environment_configuration()` to check configuration settings for each environment.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - List Elastic Beanstalk applications:
     ```bash
     aws elasticbeanstalk describe-applications
     ```
   - For each application, determine the TLS settings:
     ```bash
     aws elasticbeanstalk describe-configuration-settings --application-name <app-name> --environment-name <env-name>
     ```

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Applications in AWS Elastic Beanstalk and their respective configurations.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check the `MinTlsVersion` setting in the environment configuration details.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The control passes if the `MinTlsVersion` setting equals `1.2` or higher.
   - If it is below `1.2`, the control fails.

6. **Example:**
   - Validate that the minimum TLS version for each application and its environments is set correctly by checking the output of the environment configuration to confirm that `MinTlsVersion` is either `1.2` or `1.3`.
